Title: Verify offset transaction date is persisted from SQ1 effective date
Description: Validate that a PolicyChange offset transaction stores TRANS_DT from policy_version_effective_date and not from the prior transaction date.
Preconditions: A policy change transaction set exists with prior transaction data and a valid policy_version_effective_date supplied from SQ1.
Test Steps: 1. Create a PolicyChange transaction with TRANS_OFFSET_FL = Y. 2. Provide policy_version_effective_date = 2026-08-01. 3. Process the transaction. 4. Query the transaction table and compare the stored TRANS_DT with the incoming effective date and the prior transaction values.
Expected Results: The stored database record shows TRANS_DT = 2026-08-01 for the offset transaction, and the value is not derived from the previous transaction's TRANS_DT or TRANS_EFF_DT.

Title: Verify future-dated offset transaction remains aligned to the same future date
Description: Confirm that a future-dated policy change uses the future effective date for both the offset transaction and the corresponding onset transaction.
Preconditions: A future-dated policy change scenario is available and the effective date from SQ1 is set to a date in the future.
Test Steps: 1. Create a PolicyChange scenario with TRANS_OFFSET_FL = Y and a future-dated policy_version_effective_date. 2. Process the transaction. 3. Query the database for both the onset and offset transaction records. 4. Compare the TRANS_DT values for both rows.
Expected Results: Both related policy change transactions are stored with the same future TRANS_DT, preventing a mismatch between onset and offset timing.

Title: Verify onset transaction remains correctly stored after the fix
Description: Ensure the onset transaction record remains correct and is not altered by the offset-date logic change.
Preconditions: A PolicyChange scenario exists where both onset and offset records are created during processing.
Test Steps: 1. Create a PolicyChange transaction with an expected future effective date from SQ1. 2. Process the transaction. 3. Retrieve the onset transaction record from the database. 4. Compare the effective date fields against the expected business date.
Expected Results: The onset transaction record is stored with the correct effective date and remains consistent with the intended policy change timing.

Title: Verify MTO-related premium timing is driven by the aligned transaction dates
Description: Validate that the database records support correct premium timing for future-dated policy changes in downstream processing.
Preconditions: The system has workflow integration enabled for MTO processing and a future-dated policy change transaction is available.
Test Steps: 1. Process a future-dated PolicyChange transaction. 2. Retrieve the transaction records from the database. 3. Review the effective dates used by downstream premium processing. 4. Compare the stored dates with the intended premium trigger date.
Expected Results: The database reflects aligned transaction dates so downstream premium processing is triggered at the correct future date and no timing gap is introduced.

Title: Verify submission transactions remain unchanged
Description: Confirm that the new date-handling logic does not alter Submission transaction records.
Preconditions: A Submission transaction is available in the test environment and existing transaction data is known.
Test Steps: 1. Create a Submission transaction. 2. Process it using the current logic. 3. Query the database for the submitted transaction record. 4. Compare the stored transaction date values with the pre-change baseline.
Expected Results: The Submission transaction record remains unchanged and the fix does not alter its date handling or premium behavior.

Title: Validate that the system rejects missing effective date for offset transactions
Description: Check that incomplete policy change data is handled through validation rather than being stored with an incorrect date.
Preconditions: A PolicyChange offset transaction is created with missing policy_version_effective_date in the input payload.
Test Steps: 1. Submit a PolicyChange offset transaction without policy_version_effective_date. 2. Process the transaction. 3. Review validation error handling and database persistence. 4. Verify whether the transaction is rejected or flagged for correction.
Expected Results: The system prevents the transaction from being saved with an incorrect or blank date and returns a clear validation error or controlled exception.

Title: Validate that prior transaction dates are not reused when effective date is supplied
Description: Ensure data validation prevents the system from using prior transaction dates when a valid current effective date exists.
Preconditions: A PolicyChange transaction with prior transaction history and a valid current effective date is available.
Test Steps: 1. Create a PolicyChange offset transaction with a known prior transaction date. 2. Provide a new policy_version_effective_date that differs from the prior transaction's date. 3. Process the transaction. 4. Compare the stored TRANS_DT against the new effective date and the prior transaction date.
Expected Results: The system uses the new effective date and does not persist the stale prior transaction date in the offset transaction record.

Title: Validate date consistency across onset and offset records
Description: Ensure the data validation rules enforce matching effective dates for related policy change transactions.
Preconditions: A PolicyChange scenario exists for which onset and offset records are created together and the dates are expected to remain synchronized.
Test Steps: 1. Create a PolicyChange transaction with a future effective date. 2. Process it and inspect both records. 3. Compare the date fields between the onset and offset rows. 4. Review whether any inconsistent dates are allowed to persist.
Expected Results: Any mismatch between onset and offset transaction dates is detected and prevented, and the persisted records remain consistent.

Title: Validate non-policy-change transactions are excluded from the new date rule
Description: Confirm that the validation logic is restricted to PolicyChange offset transactions and does not impact other transaction types.
Preconditions: A Submission transaction and a PolicyChange transaction are available for comparison in the test environment.
Test Steps: 1. Create a Submission transaction and process it. 2. Create a PolicyChange offset transaction and process it. 3. Compare the validation behavior and resulting database entries. 4. Verify that the new rule applies only to the targeted policy change flow.
Expected Results: The validation logic only affects PolicyChange offset processing and leaves Submission and other non-policy-change transactions unaffected.
